metadata.title: Label the Five Supervisory Capacities in a Build Session with Claude Code

beats.narration_text: This is Musinique. A forty-minute build session contains exactly five supervisory moves. Each one named, each one preventing a specific failure that shipped to the student without it.

props.command: Label the Five Supervisory Capacities in a Build Session with Claude Code
props.segment: Label the Five Supervisory Capacities in a Build Session with Claude Code

beats.narration_text: The five capacities: Problem Formulation, Plausibility Auditing, Tool Orchestration, Interpretive Judgment, Executive Integration. They have always existed in competent software work — Claude makes their absence costly.

The same scaffolding principle that keeps a student from outsourcing their thinking protects a musician from outsourcing their voice. When Claude drafts, you judge. When Claude suggests a pitch angle, you decide if it's true. The tool that flags 'this is AI doing your creative work for you' is the tool that keeps your music yours.

artifactLines: The same tools the platforms use — pointed back at you.
Roll your own. Own the deck.

beats.narration_text: We run a GPA sorting session with each supervisory move explicitly labeled before it happens.

props.command:
claude "[PF] Write top_three_by_gpa with tie-breaking by last_name.
  Test set MUST include: at least one tie case (same GPA).
[PA] After output: run tie-case probe immediately.
[TO] If probe fails: re-prompt with exact failure description.
[IJ] Interpret any accuracy threshold before accepting.
[EI] Final: confirm all user needs from SDD are met."

beats.narration_text: PF: test set must include a tie case before output is accepted. PA: probe with tie case immediately after generation. TO: re-prompt with specific failure description. IJ: interpret the 85% accuracy threshold in context.

props.code:
# Labeled Build Transcript — GPA Sort Session

[PF] Before prompt: required tie case in test set
  - test_tie: Bell 3.92, Adams 3.92 -> Adams first

[PA] After output: probe with tie case IMMEDIATELY
  - Result: Bell returned first (bug found)

[TO] Re-prompt with exact failure:
  'Bell and Adams tie at 3.92, Adams should rank
   first (alphabetical), got Bell — fix sort key'

[IJ] 85% accuracy threshold in context:
  - 15% failure rate on tie cases = unacceptable
  - threshold requires domain interpretation

[EI] Final: all SDD needs confirmed satisfied
props.title: supervised_build.md

beats.narration_text: The labeled transcript: PF at the start — tie-case requirement stated. PA immediately after — tie-case probe run. The probe finds the bug. TO — re-prompt names the exact failure: Bell and Adams tie at 3.92, Adams should rank first, got Bell. IJ — the 85% accuracy figure requires domain interpretation before accepting.
We run the same build without supervisory labeling and identify the step where the unlabeled session would have shipped the bug.

props.command: claude "Run the same GPA sort build WITHOUT supervisory labels.\nAt which step would the tie-breaking bug have shipped?\nShow the unlabeled decision point where the failure entered."

beats.narration_text: Unlabeled session: no tie case in initial test set. Output accepted. Build ships. The bug appears in production when a real tie occurs. The only difference between the sessions is the PF move at the start — requiring a tie case before accepting output.
Naming the five capacities is how you practice them. The label is not the move — the label is the commitment to make the move before Claude generates.
Next: build and test a handoff condition protocol.
Musinique. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At nikbearbrown.com.
